name: Bitcoin
primaryUse: Final settlement, long-horizon self-custody, and simple native BTC transfers.
totalCirculationModel: Protocol-capped at 21,000,000 BTC.
notableDetails: Spectra uses a native Bitcoin engine rather than the generic WalletCore path helper.
The default receive path shown here is native SegWit BIP84 account 0.
Balance is fundamentally derived from spendable UTXO state, not an account balance field.

name: Bitcoin Cash
primaryUse: Peer-to-peer payments with a Bitcoin-derived UTXO model and distinct address/network rules.
totalCirculationModel: Protocol-capped at 21,000,000 BCH.
notableDetails: Bitcoin Cash keeps the Bitcoin-family UTXO model, but uses BCH-specific address validation, transaction history providers, and send logic in Spectra.
CashAddr formatting is common in user-facing BCH workflows, and Spectra normalizes BCH addresses accordingly.
Like Bitcoin, authoritative spendable state comes from confirmed and spendable UTXOs rather than an account balance field.

name: Bitcoin SV
primaryUse: Bitcoin-derived UTXO transfers on the BSV network with chain-specific address, policy, and provider behavior.
totalCirculationModel: Protocol-capped at 21,000,000 BSV.
notableDetails: Bitcoin SV keeps the Bitcoin-family UTXO accounting model while using its own network rules, providers, and explorer surface in Spectra.
Authoritative spendable balance still comes from spendable UTXOs rather than an account-style balance field.
Although it shares Bitcoin lineage, BSV support in Spectra is handled through dedicated service and engine flows rather than BTC settings.

name: Litecoin
primaryUse: Lower-fee payments and faster block cadence than Bitcoin.
totalCirculationModel: Protocol-capped at 84,000,000 LTC.
notableDetails: Shares the UTXO accounting model with Bitcoin but uses a different address and fee environment.
Spectra derives Litecoin through WalletCore and refreshes against Litecoin-specific providers.
Four-times Bitcoin's nominal supply does not change divisibility or ledger semantics.

name: Dogecoin
consensus: Proof of Work with AuxPoW merged-mining ecosystem support
primaryUse: Low-friction payments, transfers, and broad exchange support.
totalCirculationModel: No fixed terminal cap. Issuance is bounded per block and per year, but the lifetime supply is not protocol-capped.
notableDetails: Spectra tracks DOGE with extra discovery, keypool, and operational diagnostics beyond the other UTXO chains.
Because supply has no terminal cap, long-run circulation is intentionally treated here as unestimatable from a static settings page.
Like Bitcoin and Litecoin, the authoritative spendable state is the UTXO set.

name: Ethereum
primaryUse: General-purpose smart contracts, DeFi, ERC-20 tokens, and on-chain execution.
totalCirculationModel: No fixed maximum supply. Supply changes with validator issuance and fee burn, so long-run circulation is not statically estimatable.
notableDetails: BNB Chain reuses the same EVM keyspace and default derivation path family in Spectra.
ETH balances come from account state; ERC-20 balances come from token contract state.
History APIs and live balance APIs are separate concerns on EVM chains.

name: Ethereum Classic
primaryUse: Native ETC transfers and conservative EVM-compatible contract execution on the original Ethereum chain lineage.
totalCirculationModel: No fixed maximum supply. Emission declines over time under the ETC monetary policy, so live circulation changes and is treated as dynamic in this wiki.
notableDetails: Spectra routes ETC through the EVM engine stack, but with a dedicated chain context, chain ID, RPC set, and derivation path.
ETC uses the same 0x-style address format as Ethereum, while remaining a separate network with separate transaction history and fee conditions.
Tracked ERC-20 style tokens are intentionally not enabled on ETC in Spectra's current support surface; the ETC integration is native-asset focused.

name: BNB Chain
primaryUse: Lower-cost EVM execution, BEP-20 assets, and exchange-adjacent on-chain activity.
slip44CoinType: 9006 (Binance Smart Chain); Spectra currently derives BNB Chain wallets with 60 for EVM compatibility.
totalCirculationModel: Genesis supply began at 200,000,000 BNB, with protocol and ecosystem burn mechanisms targeting a lower long-run supply. Live circulation is dynamic, so this page treats it as not statically estimatable.
notableDetails: Spectra treats BNB Chain as an EVM network and routes it through the Ethereum-style engine stack.
Same address format and private-key family as Ethereum, but a distinct chain context and RPC network.
BEP-20 token handling follows the same general contract-balance logic as ERC-20.

name: Arbitrum
primaryUse: Lower-cost EVM execution, ERC-20 tokens, and Ethereum-compatible activity on Arbitrum One.
slip44CoinType: 60 (Ether); Spectra derives Arbitrum wallets with the Ethereum-compatible EVM path family.
totalCirculationModel: The native gas asset is ETH bridged from Ethereum mainnet, so supply is not a chain-native fixed-cap figure for Arbitrum itself.
notableDetails: Spectra treats Arbitrum as its own EVM chain context with chain ID 42161, separate RPC rotation, explorer links, diagnostics, and tracked ERC-20 token support.
Arbitrum uses standard 0x-style EVM addresses while remaining distinct from Ethereum mainnet, BNB Chain, and Avalanche.
ETH is the native gas asset on Arbitrum, while ARB is a tracked ERC-20 governance token rather than the gas token.

name: Optimism
primaryUse: Lower-cost EVM execution, ERC-20 tokens, and Ethereum-compatible activity on Optimism mainnet.
slip44CoinType: 60 (Ether); Spectra derives Optimism wallets with the Ethereum-compatible EVM path family.
totalCirculationModel: The native gas asset is ETH bridged from Ethereum mainnet, so supply is not a chain-native fixed-cap figure for Optimism itself.
notableDetails: Spectra treats Optimism as its own EVM chain context with chain ID 10, separate RPC rotation, explorer links, diagnostics, and tracked ERC-20 token support.
Optimism uses standard 0x-style EVM addresses while remaining distinct from Ethereum mainnet, Arbitrum, BNB Chain, and Avalanche.
ETH is the native gas asset on Optimism, while OP is a tracked ERC-20 governance token rather than the gas token.

name: Avalanche
consensus: Proof of Stake with Avalanche consensus
primaryUse: Native AVAX transfers and C-Chain EVM activity on Avalanche mainnet.
slip44CoinType: 9005 (Avalanche C-Chain); Spectra currently derives Avalanche wallets with 60 for EVM compatibility.
totalCirculationModel: AVAX supply is capped at 720,000,000 with live circulating supply changing over time as issuance and fee burn continue.
notableDetails: Spectra treats Avalanche C-Chain as an EVM network with its own chain context, chain ID, RPC catalog, and diagnostics surface.
Avalanche uses standard 0x-style EVM addresses while remaining a separate network from Ethereum and BNB Chain.
Current Spectra support is native-asset focused for AVAX rather than tracked ERC-20 style token balances.

name: Hyperliquid
primaryUse: Native HYPE transfers and HyperEVM activity on Hyperliquid mainnet.
slip44CoinType: 2457 (Hyperliquid); Spectra currently derives Hyperliquid wallets with 60 for EVM compatibility.
totalCirculationModel: HYPE circulation is dynamic over time, so this page treats live circulating supply as non-static.
notableDetails: Spectra treats Hyperliquid HyperEVM as a dedicated EVM chain context with its own chain ID, RPC catalog, explorer links, and diagnostics surface.
Hyperliquid uses standard 0x-style EVM addresses while remaining separate from Ethereum, BNB Chain, and Avalanche.
Spectra supports tracked ERC-20 balances and token history on HyperEVM alongside native HYPE balance refresh and send flows.

name: Tron
primaryUse: Transfers, TRC-20 token activity, and resource-metered smart contract execution.
totalCirculationModel: No fixed supply statement is surfaced here. Effective circulating supply changes over time, so it is treated as not statically estimatable in this wiki.
notableDetails: Tron fees are resource-based and rely on Bandwidth and Energy rather than a pure gas model.
USDT on Tron is a major real-world usage path and is handled as a tracked TRC-20 asset in Spectra.
Tron addresses are derived from the ECDSA key family but use Tron-specific address encoding.

name: Solana
consensus: Proof of Stake with Proof of History timing
primaryUse: High-throughput transfers, SPL tokens, and latency-sensitive on-chain applications.
totalCirculationModel: No fixed maximum supply. Inflation, staking economics, and unlock schedules make long-run live circulation dynamic.
notableDetails: SPL token balances live in associated token accounts, not in the owner wallet account directly.
A wallet can show correct history even when token-account balance discovery is degraded, which is why Spectra has Solana-specific fallback logic.

name: Aptos
primaryUse: Native APT transfers, Move-based applications, and account/resource-oriented on-chain execution.
totalCirculationModel: APT circulation changes over time with staking emissions, unlocks, and protocol economics, so this page treats live circulation as dynamic.
notableDetails: Aptos is closer to Sui in developer model than to EVM chains, but its on-chain state is account-and-resource based rather than object-centric.
Spectra treats Aptos as a native-asset focused chain in this build, with direct APT balance, send, history, diagnostics, and explorer support.
Aptos addresses are hex-formatted account addresses and Spectra normalizes them into lowercase 0x-prefixed form.

name: TON
consensus: Proof of Stake with validator-set finality
primaryUse: Native Toncoin transfers, jettons, and asynchronous contract interactions on The Open Network.
totalCirculationModel: Toncoin supply is dynamic over time, so this page treats live circulation as non-static.
notableDetails: TON transfers and contract execution are message-based, so wallet behavior differs from UTXO chains and synchronous EVM calls.
User-facing wallet addresses are often shown in friendly base64url format rather than raw workchain:hash form.
Spectra treats TON as a native-asset chain with dedicated balance, send, history, and diagnostics support.

name: Cardano
primaryUse: Native-asset transfers, staking, and eUTXO-based smart contract workflows.
totalCirculationModel: Protocol-capped at 45,000,000,000 ADA.
notableDetails: Cardano is not account-based in the Ethereum sense; it extends the UTXO model with richer script semantics.
The remaining reserve and reward distribution affect circulating supply over time, but the maximum supply is fixed.
Native assets on Cardano do not require ERC-20-style token contracts.

name: XRP Ledger
primaryUse: Fast payments, issued assets, and order-book based transfers.
totalCirculationModel: Fixed at 100,000,000,000 XRP total created supply.
notableDetails: No mining: all XRP was created at genesis.
XRPL uses reserve requirements to prevent ledger spam and excessive state growth.
Issued assets on XRPL differ materially from ERC-20 and SPL token models.

name: Monero
stateModel: Private output model with one-time stealth addresses
primaryUse: Private value transfer with sender, receiver, and amount obfuscation.
derivationPath: Not user-configurable in Spectra's current UI. Monero uses engine-specific wallet derivation and address construction rather than the generic WalletCore path surface.
totalCirculationModel: No fixed terminal cap. Monero uses tail emission after main emission, so long-run lifetime supply is not capped.
notableDetails: Monero uses stealth addresses, ring signatures, and confidential amounts, so ledger interpretation differs radically from transparent chains.
Because Monero is privacy-preserving by design, balance and history logic rely on Monero-specific backend assumptions.
Tail emission keeps miner incentives alive instead of letting issuance fall to zero permanently.

name: Sui
primaryUse: Move-based applications, object transfers, and asset-centric execution.
totalCirculationModel: Total supply capped at 10,000,000,000 SUI, with circulation released over time.
notableDetails: Sui is not simply account-based; assets and other resources are modeled as programmable objects.
The Move type system and object ownership rules are core to how transfers and shared state work.
Spectra handles Sui with a dedicated engine rather than treating it like an EVM clone.

name: NEAR
consensus: Proof of Stake with Doomslug / Nightshade architecture
primaryUse: Named-account transfers, smart contracts, and user-friendly account-based activity.
totalCirculationModel: No fixed terminal cap is surfaced here. Supply changes over time, so live circulation is treated as dynamic.
notableDetails: NEAR supports both human-readable named accounts and 64-character implicit accounts.
Accounts and contracts are both first-class network entities, rather than contracts being purely attached to EOAs.
Spectra uses NEAR RPC for balances and a NearBlocks-style indexed history source for transaction discovery.

name: Polkadot
primaryUse: Native DOT transfers, staking, governance, and relay-chain security participation.
totalCirculationModel: No fixed terminal cap is surfaced here. Supply changes over time through staking-era issuance and protocol economics, so live circulation is treated as dynamic.
notableDetails: Polkadot addresses use the SS58 address format instead of Ethereum-style hex addresses.
Spectra uses public Polkadot Sidecar and RPC endpoints for balance, history, endpoint diagnostics, and in-app send support.
Polkadot transfers are runtime calls signed as extrinsics rather than simple account-state updates through an EVM-style transaction model.

name: Internet Computer
primaryUse: Native ICP transfers, governance participation, and canister-powered applications.
totalCirculationModel: Supply is dynamic and tied to governance, staking, and protocol economics, so this page treats live circulation as non-static.
notableDetails: Spectra currently focuses on native ICP transfers through Internet Computer Rosetta endpoints rather than canister token standards.
ICP accounts use 64-character account identifiers rather than Ethereum-style EOAs or object-style addresses.
In-app send support signs native ICP transfers locally and broadcasts them through public Rosetta infrastructure.

name: Stellar
primaryUse: Native XLM transfers, issued-asset settlement, and low-fee payment routing on Stellar.
totalCirculationModel: No fixed terminal cap is surfaced here. Circulating supply is dynamic and partly shaped by foundation-controlled holdings and burns, so this page treats live circulation as non-static.
notableDetails: Spectra currently focuses on native XLM transfers rather than Stellar issued assets and trustline management.
Stellar uses StrKey-encoded public addresses that typically start with G, not hex or SS58-style formats.
In-app send support signs Stellar payment operations and broadcasts them through public Horizon endpoints.

name: Polygon
primaryUse: Low-fee EVM transfers, ERC-20 settlement, and DeFi on Polygon PoS.
totalCirculationModel: POL replaced MATIC as the gas/staking token following the 2024 token migration; circulating supply is dynamic.
notableDetails: Polygon PoS is fully EVM-compatible, so Spectra reuses the shared EVM client for balance, history, and send.
The native token is POL (formerly MATIC); Spectra uses POL as the displayed gas symbol.
Tracked ERC-20s on Polygon include native USDC, bridged USDT, and DAI.

name: Base
primaryUse: Low-fee Ethereum-compatible transfers and ERC-20 settlement on Base.
totalCirculationModel: Base is a Coinbase-incubated L2; the gas token is ETH bridged from Ethereum mainnet.
notableDetails: Base is built on the OP Stack and inherits Ethereum's security via fraud-proof rollup posting.
Spectra reuses the shared EVM client; only the chain ID (8453) and endpoint set differ from Ethereum.
Native USDC on Base uses contract 0x833589fCD6eDb6E08f4c7C32D4f71b54bdA02913.

name: Linea
primaryUse: ZK-secured EVM transfers and ERC-20 settlement on Linea.
totalCirculationModel: Linea is a ConsenSys zkEVM L2; gas token is bridged ETH.
notableDetails: Linea is a Type-2 zkEVM rollup, so Spectra reuses the shared EVM client unchanged.
Chain ID is 59144; the public RPC set covers ConsenSys-operated and third-party endpoints.
Native USDC on Linea uses contract 0x176211869cA2b568f2A7D4EE941E073a821EE1ff.

name: Scroll
primaryUse: ZK-secured EVM transfers and ERC-20 settlement on Scroll.
totalCirculationModel: Scroll is a community-built zkEVM L2; gas token is bridged ETH.
notableDetails: Scroll targets bytecode-level zkEVM equivalence, so Spectra reuses the shared EVM client.
Chain ID is 534352.
Native USDC on Scroll uses contract 0x06eFdBFf2a14a7c8E15944D1F4A48F9F95F663A4.

name: Blast
primaryUse: EVM transfers and yield-bearing ETH/USDB on Blast.
totalCirculationModel: Blast is an L2 with native ETH/USDB yield distribution; circulating supply is dynamic.
notableDetails: Blast is EVM-equivalent and rolls up to Ethereum; Spectra reuses the shared EVM client.
Chain ID is 81457.
Native USDB on Blast uses contract 0x4300000000000000000000000000000000000003.

name: Mantle
consensus: Modular rollup with EigenDA data availability
primaryUse: Low-fee EVM transfers and ERC-20 settlement on Mantle.
totalCirculationModel: Mantle uses MNT as its native gas/governance token (formerly BIT). Circulating supply is dynamic.
notableDetails: Mantle is fully EVM-compatible; Spectra reuses the shared EVM client.
Chain ID is 5000.
Native USDC on Mantle uses contract 0x09Bc4E0D864854c6aFB6eB9A9cdF58aC190D0dF9.
